articles.headline: India Unveils 'Digital Seva Sankalp' for Universal Access to Government Services
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Electronics and IT launched 'Digital Seva Sankalp', a comprehensive framework aimed at ensuring universal access to government services through digital means.
• The initiative focuses on last-mile connectivity, digital literacy, and the integration of various government portals into a single, user-friendly platform.
• Key components include expansion of Common Service Centres (CSCs), development of regional language interfaces, and robust cybersecurity measures.
• The program seeks to bridge the digital divide, enhance transparency, and improve efficiency in service delivery across rural and urban areas.
• A dedicated grievance redressal mechanism and citizen feedback system are integral to its implementation.
gsSubTopics: Governance, transparency and accountability
e-governance- applications, models, successes, limitations, and potential
Citizens Charters
Information & Communication Technology (ICT)
prelimsPoints: Digital Seva Sankalp launched by Ministry of Electronics and IT.
Aims for universal access to government services.
Key pillars: CSC expansion, regional language interfaces, cybersecurity.
Focus on transparency and efficiency in service delivery.
mainsPoints: Discuss the potential of Digital Seva Sankalp in transforming governance and citizen-centric service delivery, citing challenges like digital divide and data privacy.
Analyze how such initiatives align with India's vision of a digitally empowered society and knowledge economy.
Evaluate the role of digital public infrastructure in fostering inclusive growth and reducing corruption.
articles.imageDescription: Illustration of a hand holding a smartphone connected to various government service icons, symbolizing digital access.
articles.trivia: India's Aadhaar program is the world's largest biometric ID system, foundational to many digital public services.

articles.headline: Tragedy Strikes Jaisalmer: Bus Fire Kills 20, Injures 15
articles.explainer:
• A devastating bus fire near Jaisalmer, Rajasthan, resulted in the death of 20 passengers and left 15 others injured.
• The incident occurred on National Highway 68, reportedly due to a short circuit in the engine compartment which rapidly engulfed the vehicle.
• Emergency services, including local police and fire brigades, responded quickly, but the intensity of the flames made rescue efforts challenging.
• The Prime Minister's Office announced ex-gratia payments for the victims' families and directed authorities to provide all necessary medical aid to the injured.
• This incident has reignited calls for stricter enforcement of vehicle safety norms and regular fitness checks for commercial passenger vehicles.
gsSubTopics: Urbanization, their problems and their remedies
Disaster and disaster management
Road Safety
prelimsPoints: Location of incident: Jaisalmer, Rajasthan.
Cause: Suspected short circuit, rapid fire spread.
Government response: Ex-gratia announced, medical aid directed.
Highlighting need for stricter vehicle safety enforcement.
mainsPoints: Analyze the contributing factors to road accidents in India and suggest comprehensive measures to enhance road safety, including technological interventions and policy reforms.
Discuss the role of disaster management authorities at local and national levels in responding to such emergencies and the challenges they face.
Evaluate the effectiveness of current vehicle fitness testing and certification processes in preventing such incidents.
articles.imageDescription: Smoke rising from the charred remains of a bus on a highway, with emergency vehicles in the background.
articles.trivia: India accounts for about 11% of the total number of road accident deaths in the world.

articles.headline: RBI Maintains Status Quo on Repo Rate Amidst Inflation Concerns
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) concluded its bi-monthly review, deciding to keep the repo rate unchanged at 6.5%.
• This decision was largely influenced by persistent inflationary pressures, particularly in food and energy sectors, despite recent easing in global commodity prices.
• RBI Governor Shaktikanta Das emphasized the MPC's commitment to aligning inflation with the target while supporting economic growth.
• The central bank also revised its GDP growth forecast for FY26 slightly upwards, citing resilient domestic demand and government capital expenditure.
• Liquidity management measures were also discussed, with the RBI reiterating its flexible approach to ensure financial stability.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Government Budgeting
Monetary policy
prelimsPoints: Repo rate maintained at 6.5% by RBI's MPC.
Decision driven by inflation concerns, especially food and energy.
RBI Governor: Shaktikanta Das.
GDP growth forecast revised slightly upwards for FY26.
RBI's mandate includes maintaining price stability while keeping growth in mind.
mainsPoints: Analyze the challenges faced by the RBI in balancing inflation control and economic growth, particularly in the context of global economic uncertainties and supply-side shocks.
Discuss the effectiveness of monetary policy tools like the repo rate in managing inflation in a developing economy like India.
Examine the implications of the RBI's monetary policy stance on various sectors of the Indian economy, including banking, industry, and consumers.
articles.imageDescription: The Reserve Bank of India building in Mumbai, with its logo prominently displayed.
articles.trivia: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) was constituted in 2016 and comprises six members – three officials from the RBI and three external members nominated by the Central Government.

articles.headline: India-Japan Strategic Dialogue Emphasizes Indo-Pacific Cooperation and Tech Partnership
articles.explainer:
• India and Japan held their annual Strategic Dialogue, focusing on enhancing cooperation in the Indo-Pacific region and deepening technological partnerships.
• External Affairs Minister S. Jaishankar and his Japanese counterpart discussed regional security challenges, maritime domain awareness, and joint infrastructure projects.
• Both nations reiterated their commitment to a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific, emphasizing adherence to international law and peaceful resolution of disputes.
• Discussions also covered collaboration in emerging technologies, including AI, semiconductors, and green energy, with a view to building resilient supply chains.
• The dialogue underscored the growing convergence of strategic interests between the two democratic powers in the face of evolving geopolitical dynamics.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
prelimsPoints: India-Japan Strategic Dialogue held.
Key focus areas: Indo-Pacific cooperation, technology partnership, regional security.
Commitment to a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific.
Collaboration in AI, semiconductors, green energy mentioned.
mainsPoints: Analyze the significance of the India-Japan strategic partnership in shaping the geopolitical landscape of the Indo-Pacific region.
Discuss how bilateral cooperation in technology and infrastructure contributes to India's economic development and strategic autonomy.
Evaluate the challenges and opportunities for India and Japan in countering regional hegemonies and promoting a rules-based international order.
articles.imageDescription: Indian and Japanese flags displayed together during a diplomatic meeting.
articles.trivia: Japan is one of the few countries with which India has a '2+2' Ministerial Dialogue (Defence and Foreign Ministers).

articles.headline: Namami Gange Mission Enters Phase III, Focus on Tributary Clean-up and Sustainable Practices
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Jal Shakti announced the commencement of Phase III of the Namami Gange Mission, expanding its scope beyond the main stem of the Ganga to include its major tributaries.
• This phase will prioritize the rejuvenation of rivers like Yamuna, Ramganga, and Kali, with dedicated projects for sewage treatment, industrial effluent management, and solid waste disposal.
• A strong emphasis will be placed on community participation, local governance involvement, and the adoption of sustainable agricultural practices in the river basins.
• New technologies for real-time water quality monitoring and advanced sewage treatment plants (STPs) will be deployed across identified stretches.
• The mission aims to foster a 'Ganga Arth Ganga' model, integrating economic development with ecological restoration to create a sustainable river economy.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Water resources
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors
prelimsPoints: Namami Gange Mission Phase III announced.
Focus on Ganga tributaries: Yamuna, Ramganga, Kali.
Key aspects: Sewage treatment, industrial effluent management, community participation.
Introduction of 'Ganga Arth Ganga' model.
Ministry responsible: Ministry of Jal Shakti.
mainsPoints: Analyze the evolution of the Namami Gange Mission and the challenges faced in its previous phases. Discuss how Phase III addresses these issues.
Evaluate the 'Arth Ganga' concept as a sustainable model for river basin management, considering its economic and ecological dimensions.
Examine the role of technology and community engagement in achieving effective river rejuvenation and pollution control.
articles.imageDescription: A clean stretch of the Ganges river with ghats in the foreground, symbolizing rejuvenation efforts.
articles.trivia: The Ganga river basin is the most populous river basin in the world, home to over 500 million people.

articles.headline: ISRO Prepares for Chandrayaan-4, Focus on Lunar Sample Return Mission
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) announced accelerated preparations for its ambitious Chandrayaan-4 mission, slated for launch in 2028.
• Unlike previous lunar missions, Chandrayaan-4 will be India's first attempt at a lunar sample return mission, aiming to bring back lunar soil and rock samples to Earth.
• The mission architecture involves a lander, an ascent module, an orbiter, and a re-entry module, showcasing advanced indigenous technological capabilities.
• Scientists anticipate significant insights into the Moon's geological evolution, resource potential, and the early solar system from the returned samples.
• This mission underscores India's growing prowess in deep space exploration and its commitment to contributing to global lunar science.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Space technology
prelimsPoints: Chandrayaan-4: ISRO's next lunar mission.
Primary objective: Lunar sample return mission (first for India).
Planned launch year: 2028.
Mission architecture includes lander, ascent module, orbiter, re-entry module.
Aims to provide insights into Moon's geology and resources.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of lunar sample return missions in advancing our understanding of planetary science and potential resource utilization.
Analyze how ISRO's Chandrayaan program contributes to India's scientific capabilities, technological self-reliance, and international collaborations in space.
Examine the challenges involved in designing and executing a complex lunar sample return mission and how ISRO is addressing them.
articles.imageDescription: Artistic rendering of a lunar lander deploying a rover on the Moon's surface, with Earth visible in the distance.
articles.trivia: Only three countries have successfully returned samples from the Moon: USA, Russia (then Soviet Union), and China.

articles.headline: India Reaffirms Commitment to Indus Waters Treaty, Urges Pakistan for Dialogue
articles.explainer:
• India reiterated its commitment to the Indus Waters Treaty (IWT) during a press briefing, while also urging Pakistan to engage in constructive dialogue on pending issues.
• The statement came amidst ongoing technical discussions regarding the design of certain Indian hydropower projects on the western rivers.
• India emphasized its rights under the treaty to utilize the waters of the western rivers for non-consumptive purposes like hydropower generation, within treaty provisions.
• The Ministry of External Affairs spokesperson highlighted India's consistent track record of adherence to the IWT, despite past disagreements.
• The treaty, brokered by the World Bank, governs the sharing of waters of the Indus River system between India and Pakistan.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
prelimsPoints: India reaffirms commitment to Indus Waters Treaty (IWT).
IWT governs water sharing between India and Pakistan.
Treaty brokered by the World Bank.
India emphasizes rights for non-consumptive use on western rivers (Indus, Jhelum, Chenab).
Eastern rivers (Ravi, Beas, Sutlej) allocated to India.
mainsPoints: Discuss the historical context and key provisions of the Indus Waters Treaty and its significance in India-Pakistan relations.
Analyze the challenges and controversies surrounding the implementation of the IWT, particularly concerning hydropower projects and dispute resolution mechanisms.
Evaluate India's strategy in navigating water-sharing agreements with riparian states and its implications for regional stability.
articles.imageDescription: Map showing the Indus River system and the division of rivers between India and Pakistan.
articles.trivia: The Indus Waters Treaty is often cited as one of the most successful water-sharing treaties in the world, surviving multiple conflicts between India and Pakistan.

articles.headline: National Logistics Policy Review Highlights Infrastructure Gaps and Efficiency Gains
articles.explainer:
• A mid-term review of the National Logistics Policy (NLP) indicated significant progress in improving India's logistics efficiency but also highlighted persistent infrastructure gaps.
• The review, conducted by the Ministry of Commerce and Industry, noted improvements in multimodal connectivity and reduced transit times due to initiatives like PM Gati Shakti.
• Key challenges identified include the need for greater last-mile connectivity, modernization of warehousing facilities, and skill development in the logistics sector.
• The government plans to intensify efforts in digitalization of logistics processes and promoting greater private sector investment in logistics infrastructure.
• The NLP aims to reduce logistics costs as a percentage of GDP, making Indian goods more competitive globally.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
prelimsPoints: Mid-term review of National Logistics Policy (NLP) conducted by Ministry of Commerce and Industry.
NLP aims to reduce logistics costs as percentage of GDP.
Progress noted in multimodal connectivity (PM Gati Shakti).
Challenges: Last-mile connectivity, warehousing modernization, skill development.
mainsPoints: Discuss the objectives and impact of the National Logistics Policy on India's economic competitiveness and supply chain efficiency.
Analyze the role of infrastructure development initiatives like PM Gati Shakti in transforming India's logistics sector.
Examine the challenges in achieving a cost-effective and efficient logistics ecosystem in India and suggest policy measures to overcome them.
articles.imageDescription: A graphic illustrating various modes of transport (road, rail, sea, air) connected in a logistics network.
articles.trivia: India's logistics costs are estimated to be around 13-14% of GDP, significantly higher than global benchmarks of 8-10%.

articles.headline: Supreme Court to Hear Petitions Challenging Key Provisions of Digital Personal Data Protection Act
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court announced it would commence hearings on a batch of petitions challenging several provisions of the recently enacted Digital Personal Data Protection (DPDP) Act, 2023.
• Petitioners argue that certain clauses, particularly those related to government exemptions and data portability, may infringe upon the fundamental right to privacy and freedom of speech.
• The government, in its affidavit, defended the Act as a crucial step towards safeguarding citizens' data while balancing national security and public interest.
• The outcome of these hearings could significantly impact the implementation and interpretation of India's comprehensive data protection framework.
• Legal experts highlight the importance of the court's judgment in setting precedents for digital rights and state surveillance in the evolving digital landscape.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure
Right to privacy
Cyber security
prelimsPoints: Supreme Court to hear challenges against DPDP Act, 2023.
Concerns raised over government exemptions and data portability clauses.
Government defends Act as crucial for data safeguarding, national security.
Act aims to provide comprehensive data protection framework in India.
mainsPoints: Analyze the constitutional validity of the Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023, particularly in light of the Right to Privacy judgment (Puttaswamy case).
Discuss the challenges in balancing national security interests with individual privacy rights in the digital age, using the DPDP Act as a case study.
Evaluate the potential impact of the Supreme Court's decision on India's digital economy, innovation, and global standing in data governance.
articles.imageDescription: The Supreme Court of India building in New Delhi, symbolizing judicial review.
articles.trivia: The Puttaswamy judgment (2017) declared the right to privacy as a fundamental right under Article 21 of the Indian Constitution.

articles.headline: Global Health Summit Calls for Enhanced Pandemic Preparedness and Equitable Vaccine Access
articles.explainer:
• A major Global Health Summit concluded with a joint declaration emphasizing the urgent need for enhanced pandemic preparedness and equitable access to vaccines and treatments.
• World leaders and health organizations stressed the importance of strengthening global health infrastructure, early warning systems, and collaborative research initiatives.
• India, represented by its Health Minister, highlighted its commitment to vaccine diplomacy and its role as a global pharmaceutical hub in future pandemic responses.
• The summit called for reforms in international health regulations and greater funding for low-income countries to build resilient health systems.
• Discussions also touched upon addressing the root causes of pandemics, including climate change, biodiversity loss, and antimicrobial resistance.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
prelimsPoints: Global Health Summit concluded with focus on pandemic preparedness.
Key themes: equitable vaccine access, strengthening health infrastructure, early warning systems.
India's role: vaccine diplomacy, pharmaceutical hub.
Call for reforms in international health regulations and funding for low-income countries.
mainsPoints: Discuss the lessons learned from recent pandemics regarding global health governance and the necessity of a 'One Health' approach.
Analyze India's potential and responsibilities as a global pharmaceutical supplier and its role in promoting vaccine equity.
Examine the challenges in achieving universal health coverage and resilient health systems, especially in the context of climate change and emerging infectious diseases.
articles.imageDescription: A stylized globe surrounded by medical symbols, representing global health cooperation.
articles.trivia: The 'One Health' concept recognizes that the health of people is closely connected to the health of animals and our shared environment.

articles.headline: Government Launches National Smart Farming Initiative for Climate-Resilient Agriculture
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers' Welfare launched the 'National Smart Farming Initiative' to promote climate-resilient and sustainable agricultural practices.
• The initiative integrates IoT, AI, and remote sensing technologies for precision agriculture, crop monitoring, and efficient resource management.
• It aims to provide farmers with real-time data on soil health, weather patterns, and pest outbreaks, enabling informed decision-making.
• Pilot projects will be rolled out in drought-prone and flood-prone regions to demonstrate the efficacy of smart farming techniques in mitigating climate risks.
• The program also includes capacity building for farmers and agricultural extension workers on the adoption of advanced farming technologies.
gsSubTopics: Major crops cropping patterns in various parts of the country, different types of irrigation and irrigation system storage, transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints; e-technology in the aid of farmers
Food processing and related industries in India- scope’ and significance, location, upstream and downstream requirements, supply chain management

Issues relating to direct and indirect farm subsidies and minimum support prices; Public Distribution System- objectives, functioning, limitations, revamping; issues of buffer stocks and food security; Technology missions; economics of animal-rearing.
prelimsPoints: National Smart Farming Initiative launched by Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers' Welfare.
Aims to promote climate-resilient and sustainable agriculture.
Utilizes IoT, AI, remote sensing for precision agriculture.
Provides real-time data on soil, weather, pests.
Includes capacity building for farmers.
mainsPoints: Discuss the potential of smart farming technologies in addressing the challenges of climate change and food security in India.
Analyze the role of government initiatives in promoting the adoption of advanced agricultural technologies among small and marginal farmers.
Examine the socio-economic implications of digitalizing agriculture, including issues of digital literacy, data ownership, and equitable access.
articles.imageDescription: A drone flying over a vast agricultural field, symbolizing technology in farming.
articles.trivia: India is the second-largest producer of agricultural products worldwide.

articles.headline: Indian Ocean Rim Association (IORA) Summit Discusses Blue Economy and Maritime Security
articles.explainer:
• The annual summit of the Indian Ocean Rim Association (IORA) commenced, bringing together member states to discuss cooperation in the Blue Economy and maritime security.
• India, a prominent member, advocated for enhanced regional collaboration in sustainable ocean resource management, disaster risk reduction, and combating illegal fishing.
• Delegates emphasized the importance of maintaining a free and open Indian Ocean, crucial for global trade and energy security.
• Discussions also focused on developing robust regional mechanisms for humanitarian assistance and disaster relief (HADR) operations.
• The summit aims to strengthen economic cooperation and foster a rules-based maritime order in the strategically vital Indian Ocean region.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate
India and its neighborhood- relations
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
prelimsPoints: IORA Summit discusses Blue Economy and maritime security.
India advocates for sustainable ocean resource management, disaster risk reduction.
Focus on free and open Indian Ocean for global trade.
Discussions on HADR operations and regional cooperation.
IORA is a regional forum of Indian Ocean littoral states.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic significance of the Indian Ocean region for India and the role of IORA in promoting regional cooperation and stability.
Discuss the concept of 'Blue Economy' and its potential for sustainable development and economic growth for Indian Ocean littoral states.
Examine the challenges to maritime security in the Indian Ocean and the collective efforts required to address them, including piracy, illegal fishing, and environmental threats.
articles.imageDescription: A map of the Indian Ocean with various countries highlighted, symbolizing regional cooperation.
articles.trivia: IORA was established in 1997 and currently has 23 member states and 10 dialogue partners.

articles.headline: Indian Scientists Achieve Breakthrough in Indigenous mRNA Vaccine Technology
articles.explainer:
• Indian scientists at the National Institute of Immunology announced a significant breakthrough in developing a fully indigenous mRNA vaccine technology platform.
• This platform offers the potential for rapid development of vaccines against emerging infectious diseases, including new variants of existing viruses.
• The research focused on optimizing mRNA delivery systems and enhancing vaccine stability under diverse temperature conditions, crucial for India's public health infrastructure.
• This achievement marks a major step towards vaccine self-reliance and positions India as a key player in global vaccine innovation.
• Clinical trials for a prototype vaccine developed using this platform are expected to commence within the next year, targeting a common respiratory virus.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Health
prelimsPoints: Indian scientists develop indigenous mRNA vaccine technology platform.
Breakthrough by National Institute of Immunology.
Aims for rapid vaccine development against infectious diseases.
Focus on optimizing delivery and stability (cold chain).
Marks step towards vaccine self-reliance.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of indigenous mRNA vaccine technology for India's public health security and its role in global pandemic preparedness.
Analyze the challenges in vaccine development and distribution in a diverse country like India and how technological advancements can address them.
Evaluate the potential of biotechnology and genomics in revolutionizing healthcare and medicine in the coming decades, with a focus on ethical considerations.
articles.imageDescription: Microscopic view of mRNA strands, symbolizing advanced vaccine research.
articles.trivia: mRNA vaccines work by teaching our cells how to make a protein (or even just a piece of a protein) that triggers an immune response inside our bodies.

articles.headline: Ministry of Culture Expands 'Adopt a Heritage' Scheme to Include Digital Heritage
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Culture announced the expansion of its 'Adopt a Heritage' scheme, now encompassing digital heritage assets alongside physical monuments.
• The revamped scheme aims to engage corporate entities and individuals in the preservation and digitization of India's vast cultural and historical records.
• This includes digital archiving of ancient manuscripts, historical photographs, and audio-visual recordings, making them accessible to a global audience.
• The initiative seeks to leverage private sector expertise and CSR funds for the long-term sustainability of heritage conservation efforts.
• It also emphasizes skill development for local communities in digital preservation techniques, fostering local employment and awareness.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times
Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
prelimsPoints: Ministry of Culture expands 'Adopt a Heritage' scheme.
Now includes digital heritage assets.
Aims to digitize manuscripts, photos, audio-visuals.
Leverages corporate and individual participation (CSR funds).
Focus on skill development in digital preservation.
mainsPoints: Discuss the evolving concept of heritage conservation in the digital age and the significance of including digital assets in preservation initiatives.
Analyze the role of public-private partnerships (PPP) and Corporate Social Responsibility (CSR) in funding and executing cultural heritage projects in India.
Examine the challenges and opportunities in digitizing and making India's vast cultural heritage accessible globally, considering issues of intellectual property and digital divide.
articles.imageDescription: An ancient manuscript being scanned into a digital format, symbolizing digital heritage preservation.
articles.trivia: The original 'Adopt a Heritage: Apni Dharohar, Apni Pehchaan' scheme was launched in 2017 to involve private players in maintaining historical sites.

articles.headline: National Urban Flood Resilience Mission Launched to Tackle Climate-Induced Flooding
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs launched the 'National Urban Flood Resilience Mission' to address the growing threat of climate-induced urban flooding across Indian cities.
• The mission adopts an integrated approach, focusing on improving urban drainage systems, promoting permeable infrastructure, and developing early warning systems.
• It will also involve retrofitting existing infrastructure, conducting vulnerability assessments, and implementing nature-based solutions like urban wetlands and green spaces.
• Funding will be provided to selected cities for preparing comprehensive urban flood management plans and implementing pilot projects.
• The initiative underscores the government's commitment to making Indian cities more resilient to extreme weather events and enhancing urban sustainability.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Disaster and disaster management
Urbanization, their problems and their remedies
tags: Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs
prelimsPoints: National Urban Flood Resilience Mission launched by Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs.
Aims to tackle climate-induced urban flooding.
Integrated approach: drainage, permeable infrastructure, early warning systems.
Includes retrofitting, vulnerability assessments, nature-based solutions.
Focus on making cities resilient to extreme weather.
mainsPoints: Discuss the primary causes of urban flooding in India and the multi-faceted challenges faced by city administrations in managing them.
Analyze the effectiveness of an integrated approach to urban flood resilience, incorporating both engineering solutions and nature-based solutions.
Evaluate the role of urban planning, governance, and citizen participation in building climate-resilient cities in India.
articles.imageDescription: An aerial view of a city experiencing heavy rainfall and waterlogged streets, with green infrastructure elements visible.
articles.trivia: Mumbai's Mithi River, once a clean river, became a sewage drain and a major contributor to urban flooding during heavy monsoons.

articles.headline: BharatNet Phase III Expansion Approved, Aims for Universal Rural Broadband Connectivity
articles.explainer:
• The Union Cabinet approved the detailed project report for Phase III of the BharatNet project, focusing on achieving universal rural broadband connectivity by 2027.
• This phase will significantly expand the optical fiber network to cover all remaining gram panchayats and villages, utilizing a mix of fiber, satellite, and wireless technologies.
• Special emphasis will be placed on geographically challenging and remote areas, ensuring equitable access to digital services.
• The project aims to empower rural communities with high-speed internet for education, healthcare, e-governance, and economic opportunities.
• A robust public-private partnership (PPP) model is envisioned for the implementation and maintenance of the expanded network.
gsSubTopics: Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
E-technology in the aid of farmers
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
prelimsPoints: Union Cabinet approves BharatNet Phase III DPR.
Aim: universal rural broadband connectivity by 2027.
Covers remaining gram panchayats and villages.
Utilizes fiber, satellite, wireless technologies.
Envisions public-private partnership model.
mainsPoints: Discuss the objectives and challenges faced by the BharatNet project in achieving digital inclusion across rural India.
Analyze the socio-economic impact of universal rural broadband connectivity on education, healthcare, and economic empowerment in remote areas.
Evaluate the role of public-private partnerships in accelerating infrastructure development and ensuring sustainable service delivery in large-scale government projects.
articles.imageDescription: A network of optical fiber cables extending into a rural landscape, symbolizing connectivity.
articles.trivia: BharatNet is one of the world's largest rural broadband connectivity programs, funded by the Universal Service Obligation Fund (USOF).

articles.headline: Parliamentary Committee Recommends Reforms in Judicial Appointments Process
articles.explainer:
• A Parliamentary Standing Committee on Law and Justice submitted a report recommending significant reforms in the judicial appointments process for higher judiciary.
• The report advocated for a more transparent and merit-based system, suggesting a greater role for the executive and a structured selection process involving a diverse panel.
• It also proposed clear eligibility criteria, age limits for appointments, and a mechanism for evaluating judicial performance.
• The committee emphasized the need to address issues of judicial vacancies, diversity, and accountability while upholding judicial independence.
• These recommendations could potentially reignite the debate surrounding the collegium system and the National Judicial Appointments Commission (NJAC).
gsSubTopics: Structure, organization and functioning of the Executive and the Judiciary
Appointment to various Constitutional posts, powers, functions and responsibilities of various Constitutional Bodies
Separation of powers between various organs dispute redressal mechanisms and institutions
prelimsPoints: Parliamentary Standing Committee on Law and Justice recommends judicial appointment reforms.
Advocates for transparent, merit-based system.
Proposes greater executive role, structured selection, eligibility criteria, performance evaluation.
Aims to address vacancies, diversity, accountability.
Could reignite debate on Collegium vs. NJAC.
mainsPoints: Critically examine the functioning of the Collegium system for judicial appointments in India, highlighting its merits and demerits.
Discuss the historical context and constitutional debates surrounding judicial appointments in India, including the NJAC controversy.
Suggest comprehensive reforms to ensure transparency, accountability, and meritocracy in judicial appointments while safeguarding the independence of the judiciary.
articles.imageDescription: A gavel and legal books on a desk, symbolizing judicial proceedings and reforms.
articles.trivia: The Supreme Court in 2015 struck down the National Judicial Appointments Commission (NJAC) Act, restoring the Collegium system.

articles.headline: UN Report Highlights Slow Progress on Global Fossil Fuel Phase-out Targets
articles.explainer:
• A new report by the United Nations Environment Programme (UNEP) indicated that global progress on phasing out fossil fuels is lagging significantly behind climate targets.
• The report warned that current national commitments are insufficient to limit global warming to 1.5°C, necessitating more ambitious policies and accelerated transition to renewables.
• It highlighted the continued investment in new fossil fuel projects by major economies, undermining efforts to achieve net-zero emissions.
• India's efforts in renewable energy expansion were acknowledged, but the report also urged greater clarity on its long-term fossil fuel reduction strategy.
• The findings are expected to intensify pressure on governments ahead of the next major climate conference (COP30).
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Climate Change
Renewable energy
prelimsPoints: UNEP report on fossil fuel phase-out progress.
Highlights lagging progress, insufficient national commitments for 1.5°C target.
Criticizes continued investment in new fossil fuel projects.
India's renewable energy efforts acknowledged; urges clarity on long-term strategy.
Findings to impact discussions at COP30.
mainsPoints: Analyze the challenges and opportunities for India in transitioning away from fossil fuels while ensuring energy security and economic growth.
Discuss the concept of 'common but differentiated responsibilities and respective capabilities' in the context of global climate action and fossil fuel phase-out.
Evaluate the role of international reports and multilateral climate conferences (COPs) in influencing national climate policies and driving global environmental governance.
articles.imageDescription: A power plant emitting smoke, contrasted with wind turbines and solar panels, symbolizing the energy transition challenge.
articles.trivia: The Intergovernmental Panel on Climate Change (IPCC) is the leading international body for assessing climate change.

articles.headline: National Digital University to Expand Course Offerings and Regional Language Access
articles.explainer:
• The National Digital University (NDU) announced plans to significantly expand its course offerings and enhance accessibility through regional language interfaces.
• The expansion aims to cater to a wider student base, particularly in remote and underserved areas, promoting equitable access to higher education.
• New programs in emerging fields like Artificial Intelligence, Data Science, Sustainable Development, and specialized vocational courses will be introduced.
• The NDU is also collaborating with state governments and local educational bodies to integrate its digital learning modules into existing educational frameworks.
• This initiative aligns with the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020's vision of flexible learning pathways and leveraging technology for educational outreach.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Education
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
prelimsPoints: National Digital University (NDU) to expand course offerings.
Focus on regional language access and remote areas.
New programs in AI, Data Science, Sustainable Development, vocational courses.
Collaboration with state governments for integration.
Aligned with National Education Policy (NEP) 2020.
mainsPoints: Discuss the potential of digital universities in democratizing higher education and bridging the access gap in India, especially for marginalized communities.
Analyze how the National Digital University aligns with the objectives of the National Education Policy 2020 and its vision for a flexible and multidisciplinary education system.
Examine the challenges in implementing large-scale digital education initiatives, including issues of digital infrastructure, faculty training, and accreditation.
articles.imageDescription: Students learning from laptops in a diverse classroom setting, symbolizing digital higher education.
articles.trivia: The idea of a National Digital University was first proposed in the Union Budget 2022-23.

articles.headline: India Crosses 150 GW Renewable Energy Capacity Milestone, Driven by Solar Growth
articles.explainer:
• India achieved a significant milestone, crossing 150 GW of installed renewable energy capacity, largely propelled by rapid expansion in the solar power sector.
• The Ministry of New and Renewable Energy announced that solar energy alone accounts for over 80 GW of this capacity, showcasing its dominance in the green energy transition.
• This achievement brings India closer to its ambitious target of 500 GW of non-fossil fuel electricity capacity by 2030.
• Government policies, including production-linked incentive (PLI) schemes and favorable financing, have been instrumental in attracting investments in the sector.
• The focus now shifts to integrating this intermittent power into the grid, developing energy storage solutions, and promoting green hydrogen production.
gsSubTopics: Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Renewable energy
articles.source: Ministry of New and Renewable Energy
prelimsPoints: India crosses 150 GW renewable energy capacity.
Solar energy contributes over 80 GW.
Target: 500 GW non-fossil fuel capacity by 2030.
Government policies (PLI) instrumental.
Future focus: grid integration, energy storage, green hydrogen.
mainsPoints: Analyze the growth trajectory of renewable energy in India and the factors contributing to its rapid expansion, particularly in the solar sector.
Discuss the challenges associated with large-scale integration of intermittent renewable energy into the national grid and the solutions being explored.
Evaluate India's role in global climate action and energy transition, considering its ambitious targets and the concept of 'energy justice'.
Examine the potential of green hydrogen as a future energy source and India's strategy for its development.
articles.imageDescription: A vast field of solar panels stretching into the horizon under a clear sky.
articles.trivia: The International Solar Alliance (ISA) was jointly launched by India and France in 2015 to promote solar energy.

articles.headline: India Advocates for a Global Cybersecurity Framework at UN Forum
articles.explainer:
• India's representative at a United Nations forum advocated for the urgent development of a comprehensive global cybersecurity framework to combat rising cyber threats.
• The Indian delegation emphasized the need for international cooperation, information sharing, and capacity building to ensure a secure and open cyberspace.
• Concerns were raised regarding state-sponsored cyber-attacks, ransomware, and the weaponization of digital technologies, calling for a unified international response.
• India highlighted its own initiatives in cybersecurity, including the National Cyber Security Strategy, and offered its expertise for collaborative efforts.
• The proposal aims to establish common norms, rules, and principles for responsible state behavior in cyberspace, preventing conflicts and promoting digital trust.
gsSubTopics: Challenges to internal security through communication networks, role of media and social networking sites in internal security challenges, basics of cyber security; money-laundering and its prevention
Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism
prelimsPoints: India advocates for global cybersecurity framework at UN forum.
Emphasizes international cooperation, information sharing, capacity building.
Concerns: state-sponsored attacks, ransomware, weaponization of digital tech.
India highlights its National Cyber Security Strategy.
Proposal for common norms for responsible state behavior in cyberspace.
mainsPoints: Discuss the evolving landscape of cyber threats and their implications for national security, critical infrastructure, and individual privacy.
Analyze India's approach to cybersecurity, including its domestic policies and its stance on international cooperation for a global framework.
Examine the challenges in establishing a universally accepted global cybersecurity framework, considering differing national interests and technological capabilities.
articles.imageDescription: A digital lock icon with a globe in the background, symbolizing global cybersecurity.
articles.trivia: India's Computer Emergency Response Team (CERT-In) is the national agency for responding to computer security incidents.

highlights: India launches 'Digital Seva Sankalp' for universal access to government services, aiming to bridge the digital divide.
A tragic bus fire in Jaisalmer claims 20 lives, reigniting calls for stricter road safety norms and vehicle fitness checks.
RBI's Monetary Policy Committee maintains status quo on repo rate at 6.5% amidst persistent inflation concerns.
ISRO accelerates preparations for Chandrayaan-4, India's first lunar sample return mission, aiming for a 2028 launch.
India crosses 150 GW renewable energy capacity, with solar power leading the charge towards the 500 GW non-fossil fuel target by 2030.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the 'Digital Seva Sankalp' initiative, consider the following statements:
1. It aims to ensure universal access to government services through digital means.
2. It is launched by the Ministry of Finance.
3. Expansion of Common Service Centres (CSCs) is a key component of this initiative.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct as 'Digital Seva Sankalp' aims for universal access to government services through digital means. Statement 2 is incorrect; it is launched by the Ministry of Electronics and IT, not the Ministry of Finance. Statement 3 is correct as expansion of Common Service Centres (CSCs) is indeed a key component of the initiative for last-mile connectivity.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following is the primary objective of ISRO's upcoming Chandrayaan-4 mission?
options: (a) To establish a permanent human outpost on the Moon.
(b) To conduct a lunar sample return mission.
(c) To orbit the Moon and map its entire surface in high resolution.
(d) To deploy a large telescope on the far side of the Moon.
prelimsMocks.answer: (b) To conduct a lunar sample return mission.
prelimsMocks.explanation: Chandrayaan-4 will be India's first attempt at a lunar sample return mission, aiming to bring back lunar soil and rock samples to Earth for scientific study.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements regarding the Indian Ocean Rim Association (IORA):
1. It is a regional forum of Indian Ocean littoral states.
2. India is a founding member of IORA.
3. Blue Economy and Maritime Security are among its key areas of cooperation.
Which of the statements given above are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: All three statements are correct. IORA is indeed a regional forum of Indian Ocean littoral states. India is a prominent founding member, and the Blue Economy and Maritime Security are central to its agenda for cooperation among member states.

prelimsMocks.question: The 'Arth Ganga' model, recently highlighted in the context of the Namami Gange Mission Phase III, primarily aims to:
options: (a) Develop new navigational routes along the Ganges for increased trade.
(b) Promote economic development integrated with ecological restoration in the river basin.
(c) Divert Ganga waters to drought-prone regions through inter-linking projects.
(d) Establish a network of smart cities along the Ganges riverfront.
prelimsMocks.answer: (b) Promote economic development integrated with ecological restoration in the river basin.
prelimsMocks.explanation: The 'Arth Ganga' model aims to foster a sustainable river economy by integrating economic development activities with the ecological restoration and rejuvenation efforts of the Ganga river basin.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following bodies is responsible for setting the benchmark interest rate (repo rate) in India?
prelimsMocks.explanation: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) of the Reserve Bank of India is responsible for determining the policy interest rates, including the repo rate, to achieve the inflation target while keeping in mind the objective of growth.

mainsMocks.question: Critically analyze the challenges and opportunities for India in achieving universal access to government services through digital means. Discuss how initiatives like 'Digital Seva Sankalp' can address the digital divide and foster inclusive governance. (150 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Governance, transparency and accountability and e-governance
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce 'Digital Seva Sankalp' and its aim. Discuss challenges like digital literacy, infrastructure, language barriers, and data privacy. Outline opportunities such as enhanced transparency, efficiency, financial inclusion, and last-mile delivery. Conclude on the potential for inclusive growth.

mainsMocks.question: Despite significant progress in renewable energy capacity, India faces challenges in fully transitioning away from fossil fuels. Discuss these challenges and suggest policy measures to accelerate India's energy transition while ensuring energy security. (250 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Infrastructure: Energy, Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, Renewable energy
mainsMocks.approach: Start with India's renewable energy achievements (e.g., 150 GW milestone). Identify challenges: intermittency of renewables, grid integration, storage solutions, coal dependence, financing, land acquisition, just transition for fossil fuel workers. Propose measures: R&D in storage, smart grid development, green hydrogen push, carbon pricing, international collaboration, policy stability, and skill development.

mainsMocks.question: Examine the constitutional debates and judicial pronouncements surrounding the appointment of judges to the higher judiciary in India. In light of recent recommendations for reforms, discuss how a balance can be struck between judicial independence and accountability. (200 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Structure, organization and functioning of the Executive and the Judiciary; Appointment to various Constitutional posts; Separation of powers
mainsMocks.approach: Begin with the evolution of the Collegium system from the 'three judges cases'. Mention the NJAC controversy and its striking down. Discuss arguments for and against the Collegium (independence vs. transparency/accountability). Address recent parliamentary committee recommendations. Conclude on balancing judicial independence (basic structure) with the need for transparency, meritocracy, and broader consultation in appointments.

quickBytes.text: The Ministry of Electronics and IT (MeitY) launched 'Digital Seva Sankalp'.
The Repo Rate, currently at 6.5%, is the rate at which RBI lends money to commercial banks.
Chandrayaan-4 is ISRO's first planned lunar sample return mission, aiming for 2028.
The Indus Waters Treaty (IWT) was signed between India and Pakistan in 1960, brokered by the World Bank.
Namami Gange Mission Phase III includes major tributaries like Yamuna, Ramganga, and Kali.
The Digital Personal Data Protection (DPDP) Act, 2023, is India's comprehensive data protection law.
India's target is to achieve 500 GW of non-fossil fuel electricity capacity by 2030.
The 'Adopt a Heritage' scheme has been expanded by the Ministry of Culture to include digital heritage.
BharatNet project aims for universal rural broadband connectivity using optical fiber.
The Collegium System is the method of appointment and transfer of judges in the Supreme Court and High Courts.

GS1.headline: Tragedy Strikes Jaisalmer: Bus Fire Kills 20, Injures 15
GS1.explainer:
• A devastating bus fire near Jaisalmer, Rajasthan, resulted in the death of 20 passengers and left 15 others injured.
• The incident occurred on National Highway 68, reportedly due to a short circuit in the engine compartment which rapidly engulfed the vehicle.
• Emergency services, including local police and fire brigades, responded quickly, but the intensity of the flames made rescue efforts challenging.
• The Prime Minister's Office announced ex-gratia payments for the victims' families and directed authorities to provide all necessary medical aid to the injured.
• This incident has reignited calls for stricter enforcement of vehicle safety norms and regular fitness checks for commercial passenger vehicles.
gsSubTopics: Urbanization, their problems and their remedies
Disaster and disaster management
Road Safety
prelimsPoints: Location of incident: Jaisalmer, Rajasthan.
Cause: Suspected short circuit, rapid fire spread.
Government response: Ex-gratia announced, medical aid directed.
Highlighting need for stricter vehicle safety enforcement.
mainsPoints: Analyze the contributing factors to road accidents in India and suggest comprehensive measures to enhance road safety, including technological interventions and policy reforms.
Discuss the role of disaster management authorities at local and national levels in responding to such emergencies and the challenges they face.
Evaluate the effectiveness of current vehicle fitness testing and certification processes in preventing such incidents.
GS1.imageDescription: Smoke rising from the charred remains of a bus on a highway, with emergency vehicles in the background.
GS1.trivia: India accounts for about 11% of the total number of road accident deaths in the world.

GS1.headline: Ministry of Culture Expands 'Adopt a Heritage' Scheme to Include Digital Heritage
GS1.explainer:
• The Ministry of Culture announced the expansion of its 'Adopt a Heritage' scheme, now encompassing digital heritage assets alongside physical monuments.
• The revamped scheme aims to engage corporate entities and individuals in the preservation and digitization of India's vast cultural and historical records.
• This includes digital archiving of ancient manuscripts, historical photographs, and audio-visual recordings, making them accessible to a global audience.
• The initiative seeks to leverage private sector expertise and CSR funds for the long-term sustainability of heritage conservation efforts.
• It also emphasizes skill development for local communities in digital preservation techniques, fostering local employment and awareness.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times
Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
prelimsPoints: Ministry of Culture expands 'Adopt a Heritage' scheme.
Now includes digital heritage assets.
Aims to digitize manuscripts, photos, audio-visuals.
Leverages corporate and individual participation (CSR funds).
Focus on skill development in digital preservation.
mainsPoints: Discuss the evolving concept of heritage conservation in the digital age and the significance of including digital assets in preservation initiatives.
Analyze the role of public-private partnerships (PPP) and Corporate Social Responsibility (CSR) in funding and executing cultural heritage projects in India.
Examine the challenges and opportunities in digitizing and making India's vast cultural heritage accessible globally, considering issues of intellectual property and digital divide.
GS1.imageDescription: An ancient manuscript being scanned into a digital format, symbolizing digital heritage preservation.
GS1.trivia: The original 'Adopt a Heritage: Apni Dharohar, Apni Pehchaan' scheme was launched in 2017 to involve private players in maintaining historical sites.

GS2.headline: India Unveils 'Digital Seva Sankalp' for Universal Access to Government Services
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Electronics and IT launched 'Digital Seva Sankalp', a comprehensive framework aimed at ensuring universal access to government services through digital means.
• The initiative focuses on last-mile connectivity, digital literacy, and the integration of various government portals into a single, user-friendly platform.
• Key components include expansion of Common Service Centres (CSCs), development of regional language interfaces, and robust cybersecurity measures.
• The program seeks to bridge the digital divide, enhance transparency, and improve efficiency in service delivery across rural and urban areas.
• A dedicated grievance redressal mechanism and citizen feedback system are integral to its implementation.
gsSubTopics: Governance, transparency and accountability
e-governance- applications, models, successes, limitations, and potential
Citizens Charters
Information & Communication Technology (ICT)
prelimsPoints: Digital Seva Sankalp launched by Ministry of Electronics and IT.
Aims for universal access to government services.
Key pillars: CSC expansion, regional language interfaces, cybersecurity.
Focus on transparency and efficiency in service delivery.
mainsPoints: Discuss the potential of Digital Seva Sankalp in transforming governance and citizen-centric service delivery, citing challenges like digital divide and data privacy.
Analyze how such initiatives align with India's vision of a digitally empowered society and knowledge economy.
Evaluate the role of digital public infrastructure in fostering inclusive growth and reducing corruption.
GS2.imageDescription: Illustration of a hand holding a smartphone connected to various government service icons, symbolizing digital access.
GS2.trivia: India's Aadhaar program is the world's largest biometric ID system, foundational to many digital public services.

GS2.headline: India-Japan Strategic Dialogue Emphasizes Indo-Pacific Cooperation and Tech Partnership
GS2.explainer:
• India and Japan held their annual Strategic Dialogue, focusing on enhancing cooperation in the Indo-Pacific region and deepening technological partnerships.
• External Affairs Minister S. Jaishankar and his Japanese counterpart discussed regional security challenges, maritime domain awareness, and joint infrastructure projects.
• Both nations reiterated their commitment to a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific, emphasizing adherence to international law and peaceful resolution of disputes.
• Discussions also covered collaboration in emerging technologies, including AI, semiconductors, and green energy, with a view to building resilient supply chains.
• The dialogue underscored the growing convergence of strategic interests between the two democratic powers in the face of evolving geopolitical dynamics.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
prelimsPoints: India-Japan Strategic Dialogue held.
Key focus areas: Indo-Pacific cooperation, technology partnership, regional security.
Commitment to a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific.
Collaboration in AI, semiconductors, green energy mentioned.
mainsPoints: Analyze the significance of the India-Japan strategic partnership in shaping the geopolitical landscape of the Indo-Pacific region.
Discuss how bilateral cooperation in technology and infrastructure contributes to India's economic development and strategic autonomy.
Evaluate the challenges and opportunities for India and Japan in countering regional hegemonies and promoting a rules-based international order.
GS2.imageDescription: Indian and Japanese flags displayed together during a diplomatic meeting.
GS2.trivia: Japan is one of the few countries with which India has a '2+2' Ministerial Dialogue (Defence and Foreign Ministers).

GS2.headline: India Reaffirms Commitment to Indus Waters Treaty, Urges Pakistan for Dialogue
GS2.explainer:
• India reiterated its commitment to the Indus Waters Treaty (IWT) during a press briefing, while also urging Pakistan to engage in constructive dialogue on pending issues.
• The statement came amidst ongoing technical discussions regarding the design of certain Indian hydropower projects on the western rivers.
• India emphasized its rights under the treaty to utilize the waters of the western rivers for non-consumptive purposes like hydropower generation, within treaty provisions.
• The Ministry of External Affairs spokesperson highlighted India's consistent track record of adherence to the IWT, despite past disagreements.
• The treaty, brokered by the World Bank, governs the sharing of waters of the Indus River system between India and Pakistan.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
prelimsPoints: India reaffirms commitment to Indus Waters Treaty (IWT).
IWT governs water sharing between India and Pakistan.
Treaty brokered by the World Bank.
India emphasizes rights for non-consumptive use on western rivers (Indus, Jhelum, Chenab).
Eastern rivers (Ravi, Beas, Sutlej) allocated to India.
mainsPoints: Discuss the historical context and key provisions of the Indus Waters Treaty and its significance in India-Pakistan relations.
Analyze the challenges and controversies surrounding the implementation of the IWT, particularly concerning hydropower projects and dispute resolution mechanisms.
Evaluate India's strategy in navigating water-sharing agreements with riparian states and its implications for regional stability.
GS2.imageDescription: Map showing the Indus River system and the division of rivers between India and Pakistan.
GS2.trivia: The Indus Waters Treaty is often cited as one of the most successful water-sharing treaties in the world, surviving multiple conflicts between India and Pakistan.

GS2.headline: Supreme Court to Hear Petitions Challenging Key Provisions of Digital Personal Data Protection Act
GS2.explainer:
• The Supreme Court announced it would commence hearings on a batch of petitions challenging several provisions of the recently enacted Digital Personal Data Protection (DPDP) Act, 2023.
• Petitioners argue that certain clauses, particularly those related to government exemptions and data portability, may infringe upon the fundamental right to privacy and freedom of speech.
• The government, in its affidavit, defended the Act as a crucial step towards safeguarding citizens' data while balancing national security and public interest.
• The outcome of these hearings could significantly impact the implementation and interpretation of India's comprehensive data protection framework.
• Legal experts highlight the importance of the court's judgment in setting precedents for digital rights and state surveillance in the evolving digital landscape.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure
Right to privacy
Cyber security
prelimsPoints: Supreme Court to hear challenges against DPDP Act, 2023.
Concerns raised over government exemptions and data portability clauses.
Government defends Act as crucial for data safeguarding, national security.
Act aims to provide comprehensive data protection framework in India.
mainsPoints: Analyze the constitutional validity of the Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023, particularly in light of the Right to Privacy judgment (Puttaswamy case).
Discuss the challenges in balancing national security interests with individual privacy rights in the digital age, using the DPDP Act as a case study.
Evaluate the potential impact of the Supreme Court's decision on India's digital economy, innovation, and global standing in data governance.
GS2.imageDescription: The Supreme Court of India building in New Delhi, symbolizing judicial review.
GS2.trivia: The Puttaswamy judgment (2017) declared the right to privacy as a fundamental right under Article 21 of the Indian Constitution.

GS2.headline: Global Health Summit Calls for Enhanced Pandemic Preparedness and Equitable Vaccine Access
GS2.explainer:
• A major Global Health Summit concluded with a joint declaration emphasizing the urgent need for enhanced pandemic preparedness and equitable access to vaccines and treatments.
• World leaders and health organizations stressed the importance of strengthening global health infrastructure, early warning systems, and collaborative research initiatives.
• India, represented by its Health Minister, highlighted its commitment to vaccine diplomacy and its role as a global pharmaceutical hub in future pandemic responses.
• The summit called for reforms in international health regulations and greater funding for low-income countries to build resilient health systems.
• Discussions also touched upon addressing the root causes of pandemics, including climate change, biodiversity loss, and antimicrobial resistance.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
prelimsPoints: Global Health Summit concluded with focus on pandemic preparedness.
Key themes: equitable vaccine access, strengthening health infrastructure, early warning systems.
India's role: vaccine diplomacy, pharmaceutical hub.
Call for reforms in international health regulations and funding for low-income countries.
mainsPoints: Discuss the lessons learned from recent pandemics regarding global health governance and the necessity of a 'One Health' approach.
Analyze India's potential and responsibilities as a global pharmaceutical supplier and its role in promoting vaccine equity.
Examine the challenges in achieving universal health coverage and resilient health systems, especially in the context of climate change and emerging infectious diseases.
GS2.imageDescription: A stylized globe surrounded by medical symbols, representing global health cooperation.
GS2.trivia: The 'One Health' concept recognizes that the health of people is closely connected to the health of animals and our shared environment.

GS2.headline: Indian Ocean Rim Association (IORA) Summit Discusses Blue Economy and Maritime Security
GS2.explainer:
• The annual summit of the Indian Ocean Rim Association (IORA) commenced, bringing together member states to discuss cooperation in the Blue Economy and maritime security.
• India, a prominent member, advocated for enhanced regional collaboration in sustainable ocean resource management, disaster risk reduction, and combating illegal fishing.
• Delegates emphasized the importance of maintaining a free and open Indian Ocean, crucial for global trade and energy security.
• Discussions also focused on developing robust regional mechanisms for humanitarian assistance and disaster relief (HADR) operations.
• The summit aims to strengthen economic cooperation and foster a rules-based maritime order in the strategically vital Indian Ocean region.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate
India and its neighborhood- relations
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
prelimsPoints: IORA Summit discusses Blue Economy and maritime security.
India advocates for sustainable ocean resource management, disaster risk reduction.
Focus on free and open Indian Ocean for global trade.
Discussions on HADR operations and regional cooperation.
IORA is a regional forum of Indian Ocean littoral states.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic significance of the Indian Ocean region for India and the role of IORA in promoting regional cooperation and stability.
Discuss the concept of 'Blue Economy' and its potential for sustainable development and economic growth for Indian Ocean littoral states.
Examine the challenges to maritime security in the Indian Ocean and the collective efforts required to address them, including piracy, illegal fishing, and environmental threats.
GS2.imageDescription: A map of the Indian Ocean with various countries highlighted, symbolizing regional cooperation.
GS2.trivia: IORA was established in 1997 and currently has 23 member states and 10 dialogue partners.

GS2.headline: Parliamentary Committee Recommends Reforms in Judicial Appointments Process
GS2.explainer:
• A Parliamentary Standing Committee on Law and Justice submitted a report recommending significant reforms in the judicial appointments process for higher judiciary.
• The report advocated for a more transparent and merit-based system, suggesting a greater role for the executive and a structured selection process involving a diverse panel.
• It also proposed clear eligibility criteria, age limits for appointments, and a mechanism for evaluating judicial performance.
• The committee emphasized the need to address issues of judicial vacancies, diversity, and accountability while upholding judicial independence.
• These recommendations could potentially reignite the debate surrounding the collegium system and the National Judicial Appointments Commission (NJAC).
gsSubTopics: Structure, organization and functioning of the Executive and the Judiciary
Appointment to various Constitutional posts, powers, functions and responsibilities of various Constitutional Bodies
Separation of powers between various organs dispute redressal mechanisms and institutions
prelimsPoints: Parliamentary Standing Committee on Law and Justice recommends judicial appointment reforms.
Advocates for transparent, merit-based system.
Proposes greater executive role, structured selection, eligibility criteria, performance evaluation.
Aims to address vacancies, diversity, accountability.
Could reignite debate on Collegium vs. NJAC.
mainsPoints: Critically examine the functioning of the Collegium system for judicial appointments in India, highlighting its merits and demerits.
Discuss the historical context and constitutional debates surrounding judicial appointments in India, including the NJAC controversy.
Suggest comprehensive reforms to ensure transparency, accountability, and meritocracy in judicial appointments while safeguarding the independence of the judiciary.
GS2.imageDescription: A gavel and legal books on a desk, symbolizing judicial proceedings and reforms.
GS2.trivia: The Supreme Court in 2015 struck down the National Judicial Appointments Commission (NJAC) Act, restoring the Collegium system.

GS2.headline: National Digital University to Expand Course Offerings and Regional Language Access
GS2.explainer:
• The National Digital University (NDU) announced plans to significantly expand its course offerings and enhance accessibility through regional language interfaces.
• The expansion aims to cater to a wider student base, particularly in remote and underserved areas, promoting equitable access to higher education.
• New programs in emerging fields like Artificial Intelligence, Data Science, Sustainable Development, and specialized vocational courses will be introduced.
• The NDU is also collaborating with state governments and local educational bodies to integrate its digital learning modules into existing educational frameworks.
• This initiative aligns with the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020's vision of flexible learning pathways and leveraging technology for educational outreach.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Education
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
prelimsPoints: National Digital University (NDU) to expand course offerings.
Focus on regional language access and remote areas.
New programs in AI, Data Science, Sustainable Development, vocational courses.
Collaboration with state governments for integration.
Aligned with National Education Policy (NEP) 2020.
mainsPoints: Discuss the potential of digital universities in democratizing higher education and bridging the access gap in India, especially for marginalized communities.
Analyze how the National Digital University aligns with the objectives of the National Education Policy 2020 and its vision for a flexible and multidisciplinary education system.
Examine the challenges in implementing large-scale digital education initiatives, including issues of digital infrastructure, faculty training, and accreditation.
GS2.imageDescription: Students learning from laptops in a diverse classroom setting, symbolizing digital higher education.
GS2.trivia: The idea of a National Digital University was first proposed in the Union Budget 2022-23.

GS3.headline: RBI Maintains Status Quo on Repo Rate Amidst Inflation Concerns
GS3.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) concluded its bi-monthly review, deciding to keep the repo rate unchanged at 6.5%.
• This decision was largely influenced by persistent inflationary pressures, particularly in food and energy sectors, despite recent easing in global commodity prices.
• RBI Governor Shaktikanta Das emphasized the MPC's commitment to aligning inflation with the target while supporting economic growth.
• The central bank also revised its GDP growth forecast for FY26 slightly upwards, citing resilient domestic demand and government capital expenditure.
• Liquidity management measures were also discussed, with the RBI reiterating its flexible approach to ensure financial stability.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Government Budgeting
Monetary policy
prelimsPoints: Repo rate maintained at 6.5% by RBI's MPC.
Decision driven by inflation concerns, especially food and energy.
RBI Governor: Shaktikanta Das.
GDP growth forecast revised slightly upwards for FY26.
RBI's mandate includes maintaining price stability while keeping growth in mind.
mainsPoints: Analyze the challenges faced by the RBI in balancing inflation control and economic growth, particularly in the context of global economic uncertainties and supply-side shocks.
Discuss the effectiveness of monetary policy tools like the repo rate in managing inflation in a developing economy like India.
Examine the implications of the RBI's monetary policy stance on various sectors of the Indian economy, including banking, industry, and consumers.
GS3.imageDescription: The Reserve Bank of India building in Mumbai, with its logo prominently displayed.
GS3.trivia: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) was constituted in 2016 and comprises six members – three officials from the RBI and three external members nominated by the Central Government.

GS3.headline: Namami Gange Mission Enters Phase III, Focus on Tributary Clean-up and Sustainable Practices
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Jal Shakti announced the commencement of Phase III of the Namami Gange Mission, expanding its scope beyond the main stem of the Ganga to include its major tributaries.
• This phase will prioritize the rejuvenation of rivers like Yamuna, Ramganga, and Kali, with dedicated projects for sewage treatment, industrial effluent management, and solid waste disposal.
• A strong emphasis will be placed on community participation, local governance involvement, and the adoption of sustainable agricultural practices in the river basins.
• New technologies for real-time water quality monitoring and advanced sewage treatment plants (STPs) will be deployed across identified stretches.
• The mission aims to foster a 'Ganga Arth Ganga' model, integrating economic development with ecological restoration to create a sustainable river economy.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Water resources
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors
prelimsPoints: Namami Gange Mission Phase III announced.
Focus on Ganga tributaries: Yamuna, Ramganga, Kali.
Key aspects: Sewage treatment, industrial effluent management, community participation.
Introduction of 'Ganga Arth Ganga' model.
Ministry responsible: Ministry of Jal Shakti.
mainsPoints: Analyze the evolution of the Namami Gange Mission and the challenges faced in its previous phases. Discuss how Phase III addresses these issues.
Evaluate the 'Arth Ganga' concept as a sustainable model for river basin management, considering its economic and ecological dimensions.
Examine the role of technology and community engagement in achieving effective river rejuvenation and pollution control.
GS3.imageDescription: A clean stretch of the Ganges river with ghats in the foreground, symbolizing rejuvenation efforts.
GS3.trivia: The Ganga river basin is the most populous river basin in the world, home to over 500 million people.

GS3.headline: ISRO Prepares for Chandrayaan-4, Focus on Lunar Sample Return Mission
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) announced accelerated preparations for its ambitious Chandrayaan-4 mission, slated for launch in 2028.
• Unlike previous lunar missions, Chandrayaan-4 will be India's first attempt at a lunar sample return mission, aiming to bring back lunar soil and rock samples to Earth.
• The mission architecture involves a lander, an ascent module, an orbiter, and a re-entry module, showcasing advanced indigenous technological capabilities.
• Scientists anticipate significant insights into the Moon's geological evolution, resource potential, and the early solar system from the returned samples.
• This mission underscores India's growing prowess in deep space exploration and its commitment to contributing to global lunar science.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Space technology
prelimsPoints: Chandrayaan-4: ISRO's next lunar mission.
Primary objective: Lunar sample return mission (first for India).
Planned launch year: 2028.
Mission architecture includes lander, ascent module, orbiter, re-entry module.
Aims to provide insights into Moon's geology and resources.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of lunar sample return missions in advancing our understanding of planetary science and potential resource utilization.
Analyze how ISRO's Chandrayaan program contributes to India's scientific capabilities, technological self-reliance, and international collaborations in space.
Examine the challenges involved in designing and executing a complex lunar sample return mission and how ISRO is addressing them.
GS3.imageDescription: Artistic rendering of a lunar lander deploying a rover on the Moon's surface, with Earth visible in the distance.
GS3.trivia: Only three countries have successfully returned samples from the Moon: USA, Russia (then Soviet Union), and China.

GS3.headline: National Logistics Policy Review Highlights Infrastructure Gaps and Efficiency Gains
GS3.explainer:
